LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. PETITIONER THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES' PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS, AWARDING $618.75 IN REASONABLE ATTORNEYS FEES AND $510 COSTS IN CONNECTION WITH THIS MOTION, FOR A TOTAL OF $7434.67. THE COURT NEEDS A CLEAN COPY PAGE 1 OF THE PROPOSED ORDER, WHICH MAY BE EMAILED TO THIS DEPARTMENT AT CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PETITIONER THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES IN THEIR CAPACITIES AS TRUSTEES OF THE CARPENTERS HEALTH  AND WELFARE TRUST FUND FOR CALIFORNIA; CARPENTERS VACATION-HOLIDAY TRUST FUND FOR NORTHERN CALIFORNIA; CARPENTERS PENSION TRUST FUND FOR NORTHERN CALIFORNIA; CARPENTERS ANNUITY TRUST FUND FOR NORTHERN CALIFORNIA; AND CARPENTERS TRAINING TRUST FUND FOR NORTHERN CALIFORNIA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA CARPENTERS REGIONAL COUNCIL RECOVER FROM RESPONDENT WILLIAMS, EARL CURRY INDIVIDUALLY LUKATE COMPANY WILLIAMS, EARL CURRY INDV AND DBA LUKATE COMPANY $6,305.92 PRINCIPAL, $618.75 ATTORNEY FEES, $510.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $7,434.67, SEE SCANNED DOC

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Mar-20-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PETITIONER THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES' Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is Granted. No opposition filed. The Court will sign the proposed Order submitted with the moving papers, awarding $618.75 in reasonable attorneys fees and $510 costs in connection with this motion, for a total of $7434.67. The Court needs a clean copy page 1 of the proposed Order, which may be emailed to this Department at contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
